name: Redecilla del Camino - Camino Francés Loop
name_original: Redecilla, límite entre Castilla y León, Iglesia de la Virgen de la Calle – circular
description: Jog the Redecilla del Camino - Camino Francés Loop, a 4.5-mile route through Castilian grain fields and past historic village landmarks.
region_intro: Jogging around Redecilla Del Camino offers diverse terrain, situated on the French Way of the Camino de Santiago and at the base of the Sierra de la Demanda. This location provides a mix of relatively flat agricultural paths and more varied topography in the Montes de Ayago hills. The region is characterized by natural landscapes, traditional settings, and the presence of the Tirón river, creating a peaceful environment for running. Best jogging routes around Redecilla Del Camino The most popular jogging route is Redecilla del Camino - Camino Francés Loop, a 4.5 miles (7.2 km) trail that takes 46 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows a section of the historic Camino Francés. Another top favourite among local runners is Border between Castilla y León by Redecilla del Camino — circular from Castildelgado, a moderate 8.9 miles (14.3 km) path. This route offers varied scenery, including agricultural fields and views towards the border. Local runners also love the Beech Forest of GR 190 – Bonicaparra Shelter loop from Ezcaray, a 8.4 miles (13.5 km) trail leading through Beech Forest, often completed in about 1 hour 50 minutes. Jogging around Redecilla Del Camino is defined by historic Camino de Santiago paths, agricultural plains, and the rolling Montes de Ayago hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy runs to more challenging trails. The routes in Redecilla Del Camino are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 500 runners have used komoot to explore Redecilla Del Camino's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Redecilla del Camino?
A: There are over 70 running routes in the Redecilla del Camino area, offering a wide variety of options for different skill levels and preferences. These include 2 easy routes, 33 moderate routes, and 37 difficult routes, ensuring there's something for every runner.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails in Redecilla del Camino?
A: The terrain around Redecilla del Camino is quite varied. You'll find relatively flat agricultural paths, especially along sections of the historic French Way of the Camino de Santiago. For those seeking more challenge, the Montes de Ayago hills, part of the Sierra de la Demanda, offer more varied topography with inclines and scenic views.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running routes in the area?
A: Yes, Redecilla del Camino offers several easy running options. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990069645/redecilla-del-camino-camino-frances-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Redecilla del Camino - Camino Francés Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 4.5-mile (7.2 km) trail that follows a well-established section of the Camino, providing a relatively gentle experience suitable for beginners.

Q: Can I find circular running routes around Redecilla del Camino?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A great example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050010480/border-between-castilla-y-leon-by-redecilla-del-camino-circular-from-castildelgado" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Border between Castilla y León by Redecilla del Camino — circular from Castildelgado&lt;/a&gt;, an 8.9-mile (14.3 km) moderate path offering varied scenery.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while jogging in Redecilla del Camino?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty and historical points of interest. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4705206" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rock formations on the banks of the Tirón River&lt;/a&gt;, or explore the Montes de Ayago. The village itself features the Church of Nuestra Señora de la Calle and a Jurisdictional Rollo, which can be interesting focal points during your run.

Q: Are there any dog-friendly running trails in Redecilla del Camino?
A: Many of the natural trails and paths around Redecilla del Camino, particularly those along the Camino de Santiago and through agricultural areas, are suitable for running with dogs. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially near livestock or in populated areas, and to carry water for both of you.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Redecilla del Camino?
A: The spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant conditions for running in Redecilla del Camino, with milder temperatures and beautiful natural scenery. Summer can be warm, especially in the middle of the day, while winter can bring colder temperatures, particularly in the higher elevations of the Sierra de la Demanda.

Q: Are there any running routes that offer significant elevation gain for a challenge?
A: Yes, if you're looking for a more challenging run with significant elevation, the routes extending into the Montes de Ayago and towards the Sierra de la Demanda will provide this. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24312088" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beech Forest of GR 190 – Bonicaparra Shelter loop from Ezcaray&lt;/a&gt;, though difficult, features over 530 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Redecilla del Camino?
A: The running routes in Redecilla del Camino are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 7 reviews. Runners often praise the peaceful environment, the mix of historic Camino paths and natural landscapes, and the variety of options for different fitness levels.

Q: Is public transport available to access the running routes?
A: Redecilla del Camino is a small village, and while it's situated on the Camino de Santiago, public transport options directly to trailheads might be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules if you plan to rely on public transport. Many visitors arrive by car, and parking is generally available within or near the village.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running options in the area?
A: For families, the flatter sections of the Camino de Santiago offer excellent opportunities for a leisurely run or walk. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990069645/redecilla-del-camino-camino-frances-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Redecilla del Camino - Camino Francés Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a good choice, providing a relatively easy and scenic path that can be enjoyed by all ages.
tips: Beautiful church built in the 12th century and rebuilt in the 17th and 18th centuries. Its tower is square in brick and has a portico with a high Renaissance arcade presided over by a stone image of the Virgen de la Calle, from the 14th century. Inside there is a Romanesque baptismal font from the 12th century.
Quiet little town of wonderful people

The kingdoms of Castilla and León have a remote origin, sometimes separated, sometimes united, either by Muslim domination or by different personal interests of the rulers. Castilla-León is currently one of the 17 autonomous regions of Spain.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog this loop?
faqs.answer: The Redecilla del Camino - Camino Francés Loop is designed for jogging and typically takes around 45-50 minutes to complete. This estimate is based on an average jogging pace for the 7.17 km distance.

faqs.question: Is this loop suitable for beginner joggers?
faqs.answer: Yes, this loop is generally considered suitable for beginner joggers. The section of the Camino Francés around Redecilla del Camino is known for its gentle and relatively flat terrain with minimal elevation changes, making it accessible for those with average fitness. However, proper hydration and sun protection are always advisable.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for jogging on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain primarily consists of wide country paths, often dirt and gravel, which are good for jogging. You may also encounter some asphalt sections. The landscape features open country and vast grain fields typical of Castilla y León. While generally gentle, muddy sections can occur depending on recent weather.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or viewpoints can I see along the loop?
faqs.answer: Along the loop, you'll encounter several points of interest. You can enjoy a View of Redecilla del Camino itself. A significant historical site is the Historic Border Between Castile and León. Within Redecilla del Camino, don't miss the Church of Our Lady of the Street (Redecilla del Camino), which houses a magnificent 12th-century Romanesque baptismal font.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best times to jog this route. The weather is milder, and the landscape is vibrant. Summer can be very hot, especially on the exposed, treeless stretches, making early morning or late evening runs preferable. Winters can be cold, but the paths are usually still accessible.

faqs.question: Are there parking options available for this loop?
faqs.answer: Redecilla del Camino is a small village on the Camino Francés, and while there isn't a dedicated trailhead parking lot for this specific loop, you can typically find street parking within the village. As a pilgrim stop, it's accustomed to visitors.

faqs.question: Is the Redecilla del Camino - Camino Francés Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the loop is generally dog-friendly. The wide country paths are suitable for dogs. However, always ensure your dog is under control, especially when passing through villages or near agricultural areas, and be mindful of other pilgrims and local wildlife. Carry water for your dog, especially on warmer days.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to jog this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit or have to pay an entrance fee to jog the Redecilla del Camino - Camino Francés Loop. It utilizes public paths and sections of the Camino Francés, which are freely accessible.

faqs.question: Does this loop connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this loop is situated directly on the historic Camino Francés. It also intersects with sections of the Camino Rueda, Camino de los Linares, Camino del Nogal, and Camino a la Granja, offering opportunities for longer explorations if desired.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the loop?
faqs.answer: Yes, Redecilla del Camino is a traditional stop for pilgrims on the Camino Francés. You'll find pilgrim accommodations (albergues) and basic services, including cafes and small eateries, within the village and in nearby villages like Castildelgado and Viloria de Rioja.

faqs.question: What should I bring for jogging this loop?
faqs.answer: For jogging this loop, we recommend bringing appropriate running shoes, water (especially on hot days as some stretches are exposed), sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and a light jacket if jogging in cooler months. While basic services are available in Redecilla del Camino, it's always good to be prepared.
